title: PostgreSQL Migration Terrain Map
summary: Comprehensive map of the PG backend migration across packages/opencode. Schema definitions: 100% complete. Infrastructure: mostly wired. Remaining gaps: coordination tables missing PG equivalent, CI lacks PG service, drizzle.config.pg.ts untracked, app-layer imports still point to .sql.ts.

timestamps_helper.description: Shared TimestampsPg {time_created, time_updated} helper — used by all schema files via spread operator

sqlite_schema_files.description: Legacy SQLite schema files that need Postgres equivalents — all except tool/coordination.ts already have them

without_pg_equivalent.note: The archived coordination.pg.sql.ts existed in prior sessions but is MISSING from src/

infrastructure_files.description: All database infrastructure files in packages/opencode/src/storage/

db_pg.purpose: PG database init — PGlite (embedded WASM) + node-postgres Pool. Has init(), applyMigrations(), checkPgHealth()

migrate.purpose: Dual-dialect migration runner. Auto-detects SQLite vs PG via OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL. Runnable as CLI entry point

adapter.purpose: Effect DatabaseAdapter — wraps Drizzle client. Provides makeSQLiteAdapter() and makePgAdapter() with unified Interface

adapter_dual_write.purpose: PG-001: Dual-write adapter — SQLite primary + PG async shadow. Experimental — no transaction coordination

pg_pool.purpose: PG-004: Production Postgres connection pool with retry (Effect Schedule), health checks, graceful teardown

db_ts.purpose: Main DB singleton. Creates client via #db import alias (points to db.pg.ts). Provides use(), transaction(), close()

schema_ts.purpose: PG schema barrel — re-exports all pgTable instances from .pg.sql.ts files

schema_pg_ts.purpose: Identical to schema.ts — duplicate PG schema barrel (appears to be a migration artifact)

db_duckdb.purpose: DuckDB database init for analytical queries

schema_duckdb.purpose: DuckDB analytical schema — views that read from PG-based parquet/exports

storage_ts.purpose: Data migration service (file-based JSON migrations, not Drizzle schema migrations)

postgres.note: EXISTS ON DISK but NOT in git — needs to be committed

import_alias.description: The #db import subpath in package.json that routes database imports
import_alias.note: Now points to db.pg.ts for all platforms. Previously pointed to db.bun.ts (SQLite/BunSQLite)

OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL.purpose: Primary PG connection string. Set → node-postgres Pool path. Unset → PGlite embedded path

test_fixture.purpose: PG test harness with schema-per-test isolation. PGlite in-memory by default, real PG via OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL

adapter_pg_test.purpose: PG adapter integration tests using production PgAdapter with temporary PGlite data dir
adapter_pg_test.tests_count: ~15 test cases covering query, transaction, error handling, DuckDB firewall

what_is_done.description: Completed work items for the PG backend migration
items: All 8 core `.sql.ts` table files have complete `.pg.sql.ts` equivalents (17 pgTable instances total)
`drizzle.config.pg.ts` exists with `dialect: 'postgresql'` and correct schema glob
`migrate.ts` supports both SQLite and PG dialect with auto-detection via `OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL`
`db.pg.ts` has `applyMigrations()` working for both PGlite (manual SQL) and node-postgres (Drizzle migrator)
`pg-pool.ts` wraps `pg.Pool` with retry, health checks, and graceful shutdown
`#db` import alias in package.json now points to `db.pg.ts` for all platforms
PG test fixture exists with schema-per-test isolation
`DatabaseConfig` Effect service reads OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL, SSL, pool size

what_is_missing.description: Remaining gaps for the PG backend migration

items.title: coordination.pg.sql.ts missing from src/
items.description: 3 coordination tables (CoordinationClaimTable, CoordinationReservationTable, CoordinationFanOutTable) in tool/coordination.ts use sqliteTable with NO pgTable equivalent. An archived copy existed but was deleted.
items.impact: drizzle-kit generate will skip coordination tables in PG output. These are critical for cross-session coordination.
items.action: Create coordination.pg.sql.ts with pgTable equivalents for all 3 tables

items.title: drizzle.config.pg.ts not tracked in git
items.description: drizzle.config.pg.ts exists on disk but is untracked/not committed. CI cannot regenerate migrations.
items.impact: Build reproducibility failure. New clones can't generate PG DDL.

items.title: CI has no Postgres service container
items.description: 26 workflow files in .github/workflows/ — NONE reference postgres, pg services, or OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL. PG tests can't run in CI.
items.impact: PG migration tests never execute in CI. Regressions go undetected.
items.action: Add PG job to test.yml with postgres service container and OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL env

items.title: App-layer imports still use .sql.ts paths
items.description: ~30+ domain files across src/ import from drizzle-orm directly and reference .sql.ts table names. The schema.ts barrel now re-exports from .pg.sql.ts, but production code still imports sqliteTable references through other paths.
items.impact: Runtime actually uses PGlite/node-postgres Drizzle client now via #db alias, but query patterns using sqlite-specific types may fail.
items.action: Audit all domain files for sqlite-specific imports and migrate to .pg.sql.ts paths

items.title: migration-pg/ may be stale
items.description: 2 migration folders exist in migration-pg/. Need to verify they represent the full current schema state. coordination tables are missing, hinting they were generated before those tables had .pg.sql.ts equivalents.
items.impact: Applied migrations may be missing coordination tables.
items.action: Re-run drizzle-kit generate against drizzle.config.pg.ts after filling GAP-1

items.title: schema.ts and schema-pg.ts are duplicates
items.description: Both files export identical 8 re-exports from .pg.sql.ts files. One of them (schema.ts) historically exported from .sql.ts files. The duplicate should be resolved.
items.impact: Confusion about which barrel to import from. Dead code risk.
items.action: Consolidate to single schema.ts barrel, remove schema-pg.ts

items.title: No dual-dialect type safety
items.description: No compile-time check ensures .pg.sql.ts and .sql.ts equivalents stay in sync. Schema drift between dialects could cause silent data corruption.
items.impact: Long-term maintenance risk as schemas evolve independently.
items.action: Add a compile-time schema comparison script or adopt single-source-of-truth pattern

migration_lanes_status.description: Status of each named PG migration lane from the roadmaps

PG-001.title: Schema definitions + application-layer import switching
PG-001.app_layer_switching: 0% — production imports still point to .sql.ts patterns

PG-002.title: Migration runner with dual dialect support

PG-003.title: CI integration for Postgres tests
remaining: GAP-3: CI Postgres service container + workflow job

recommended_action_order.action: Create coordination.pg.sql.ts with pgTable equivalents for 3 coordination tables
Run drizzle-kit generate against drizzle.config.pg.ts to regenerate fresh PG migration
Consolidate schema.ts and schema-pg.ts into single barrel
Audit and switch app-layer imports from .sql.ts to .pg.sql.ts patterns across ~30 domain files
Add PG service container job to .github/workflows/test.yml for CI PG test execution

smoking_guns.finding: coordination.pg.sql.ts exists in session archives but NOT in src/ — 3 coordination tables have no Postgres equivalent
drizzle.config.pg.ts is untracked — CI and fresh clones lack it
smoking_guns.evidence: git status would show it as untracked; committed drizzle.config.ts still uses sqlite dialect

smoking_guns.finding: CI has zero Postgres references — PG tests never execute in pipeline
smoking_guns.evidence: All 26 workflow files surveyed; none contain 'postgres', 'pg', or 'OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL'

smoking_guns.finding: schema.ts now re-exports from .pg.sql.ts but schema-pg.ts is an identical duplicate
smoking_guns.evidence: Both files export identical 8 re-exports from .pg.sql.ts paths

drizzle_import_ecosystem.general: ~30+ domain files import helpers (eq, and, sql, etc.) from drizzle-orm

env_var_blocklist.decision: DATABASE_URL is in the env blocklist for tool subprocesses (packages/core/src/util/env-blocklist.ts:12, packages/desktop/src/main/env-blocklist.ts:12)
env_var_blocklist.note: Tools spawned by the agent won't inherit DATABASE_URL — important for security but also means (non-OPENCODE_) env var won't leak
